title: SIGSEGV crash on arm64 when podman run --arch=amd64 --systemd=always
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Crash when using qemu-x86_64-static together with podman "systemd mode". This happens even without the actual `systemd` executable.

I observe the bug while running podman on Mac M1 arm64 (via podman machine). However, it should be also reproducible directly on `fedora-coreos-36.20221014` arm64.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Use ARM64 MacOS (v12.6 Monterey)
2. Install podman, something along
    ```sh
    brew install podman
    podman machine init
    podman machine start
    ``` 
4. execute
    ```sh
    uname -a
    podman run --rm --arch=arm64 busybox echo OK1
    podman run --rm --arch=amd64 busybox echo OK2
    podman run --rm --arch=arm64 --systemd=always busybox echo OK3
    podman run --rm --arch=amd64 --systemd=always busybox echo OK4
    echo $?
    ```

**Describe the results you received:**
stdout:
```
Darwin Mac 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Aug 22 20:19:52 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.140.49~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64 arm Darwin
OK1
OK2
OK3
139
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
stdout:
```
Darwin Mac 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Aug 22 20:19:52 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.140.49~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64 arm Darwin
OK1
OK2
OK3
OK4
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
podman machine ssh cat /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-x86_64
enabled
interpreter /usr/bin/qemu-x86_64-static
flags: F
offset 0
magic 7f454c4602010100000000000000000002003e00
mask fffffffffffefe00fffffffffffffffffeffffff
```

Podman vm images name is `podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-36.20221014.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


No, but a pretty recent one

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The same crash is observed when running `podman` client from within the podman-machine VM.

comments.body: I believe this is a bug in qemu-user-static, and not likely something Podman can solve.
Do you have suggestions how I can prepare a minimal reproduction for qemu-user-static without podman to check this possibility?

BTW These all seem to work for me.
```
sh-5.2# podman run --rm --arch=arm64 busybox echo OK1
podman run --rm --arch=amd64 busybox echo OK2
podman run --rm --arch=arm64 --systemd=always busybox echo OK3
podman run --rm --arch=amd64 --systemd=always busybox echo OK4
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, I am closing.
